extension.valueMarkdown:
The PCP addresses her mild asthma (well controlled on PRN albuterol), does routine preventive care, and then discusses the screening results. Maria is receptive to help. The PCP creates a closed-loop referral to Rose City Community Services, a local community-based organization that provides food pantry access, SNAP application assistance, and transit vouchers.

The CBO receives the referral, a case worker contacts Maria, and over the following weeks connects her with food pantry enrollment and helps her apply for SNAP benefits. The Task is updated as milestones are reached, and eventually marked completed when services are delivered.

Two sites, one state (OR), one city. This tests Permission Ticket constraints on scopes — the CBO needs access only to the referral (ServiceRequest), tracking (Task), and screening results (Observation), not the patient's clinical records.

extension.valueMarkdown: Maria presents to Cascade Community Health Center as a new patient to establish primary care with Dr. Angela Morales, MD.

Maria returns for fasting blood draw. Lab results: fasting glucose 90 mg/dL, HbA1c 5.2%, total cholesterol 192, LDL 112, HDL 54, triglycerides 130, BUN 14, creatinine 0.8, sodium 140, potassium 4.1, chloride 102, CO2 24, calcium 9.4, albumin 4.2, total protein 7.0, alkaline phosphatase 58, AST 22, ALT 18, total bilirubin 0.6, WBC 6.8, hemoglobin 13.2, hematocrit 39.4%, platelets 245.
Maria presents for her first annual wellness visit at Cascade. David Kim, MA, performs intake: BP 120/76, HR 72, weight 150 lbs (BMI 26.6), temperature 98.2°F, respirations 14, SpO2 98%.

reasonCode.text: Annual wellness labs — fasting blood draw

extension.valueMarkdown: Fasting blood draw completed. Results: fasting glucose 92 mg/dL, HbA1c 5.3%, total cholesterol 196, LDL 116, HDL 52, triglycerides 140, CBC within normal limits (WBC 7.1, Hgb 13.0, Hct 39.0%, Plt 238).
Maria calls reporting 4 days of nasal congestion, sore throat, and a mild increase in albuterol use (3–4 times in the past week). No fever, no shortness of breath at rest.

reasonCode.text: Annual wellness exam; Tdap booster immunization

extension.valueMarkdown: David Kim, MA, performs intake: BP 122/78, HR 74, weight 152 lbs (BMI 26.9), temperature 98.4°F, respirations 16, SpO2 98%. PHQ-2 score 1 (negative).
Fasting labs drawn. Results: fasting glucose 88 mg/dL, HbA1c 5.2%, total cholesterol 194, LDL 114, HDL 53, triglycerides 135, CBC unremarkable (WBC 6.5, Hgb 13.4, Hct 39.8%, Plt 252).
David Kim, MA, performs intake: BP 118/76, HR 70, weight 152 lbs (BMI 26.9), temperature 98.6°F, respirations 14, SpO2 99%. PHQ-2 score 0 (negative).

reasonCode.text: Asthma exacerbation triggered by cold air and occupational dust exposure

extension.valueMarkdown: Maria requests a telemedicine visit via patient portal, reporting increased albuterol use over the past 10 days (daily use, sometimes twice daily) coinciding with a cold snap and increased dust exposure at a client's home during her home health aide work. No fever, no purulent sputum, no nocturnal awakenings.

Priya Nair, RN, calls Maria for scheduled asthma follow-up. Maria reports significant improvement — albuterol use decreased to every other day, and she has been wearing a mask during cold weather and at her dusty client's home.
David Kim, MA, performs intake: BP 120/78, HR 72, weight 154 lbs (BMI 27.3), temperature 98.4°F, respirations 16, SpO2 98%. PHQ-2 score 0 (negative).
Fasting labs drawn. Results: fasting glucose 94 mg/dL, HbA1c 5.3%, total cholesterol 200, LDL 120, HDL 50, triglycerides 150, CBC unremarkable (WBC 7.0, Hgb 12.9, Hct 38.8%, Plt 240).

reasonCode.text: Annual wellness exam; cervical cancer screening (Pap smear)

extension.valueMarkdown: Fasting labs drawn on 3/21; results returned 3/22. Lab results: fasting glucose 92 mg/dL, HbA1c 5.4%, total cholesterol 198, LDL 118, HDL 51, triglycerides 145, CBC unremarkable (WBC 6.9, Hgb 13.1, Hct 39.2%, Plt 248).
David Kim, MA, performs intake: BP 122/78, HR 76, weight 156 lbs (BMI 27.6), temperature 98.4°F, respirations 16, SpO2 97%. PHQ-2 score 1 (negative).

reasonCode.text: Nasal congestion and cough for 3 days; annual influenza vaccination

extension.valueMarkdown: Maria presents with 3 days of nasal congestion, mild cough, and low-grade temperature (reported 99.5°F at home). She also wants her annual flu shot.

reasonCode.text: Annual wellness visit, SDOH screening positive, social care referral created

extension.valueMarkdown: Maria presents for her annual wellness visit. David Kim, MA, performs intake: BP 120/76, HR 72, weight 154 lbs (BMI 27.2), temperature 98.4°F, respirations 16, SpO2 98%.

Fasting blood draw completed two days after annual visit. Lab results: fasting glucose 94 mg/dL, HbA1c 5.4%, total cholesterol 198, LDL 118, HDL 52, triglycerides 140, CBC unremarkable (WBC 6.8, Hgb 13.2, Hct 39.4%, Plt 245), CMP within normal limits (BUN 13, creatinine 0.8, sodium 139, potassium 4.0, chloride 101, CO2 25, calcium 9.5, albumin 4.1, total protein 7.1, ALP 55, AST 20, ALT 17, total bilirubin 0.5).

Priya Nair, RN (care coordinator at Cascade), calls Maria by telephone to check on the status of the social care referral to Rose City Community Services. Maria confirms that Janet Flores from Rose City contacted her the previous week and they have already had an intake call.

reasonCode.text: Social care referral closure, outcomes confirmed

extension.valueMarkdown: Dr. Morales conducts a brief telephone encounter with Maria to confirm the outcomes of the social care referral.

code.text: Referral to Rose City Community Services for food assistance navigation and transportation support
